text_original: Motse said: Now, all tie rulers desire tieir provinces to be wealthy, their people to be numerous, and their jurisdiction to secure order.
text_normalized: Motse said: Now, all tie rulers desire tieir provinces to be wealthy, their people to be numerous, and their jurisdiction to secure order.

text_original: But what they obtain is not wealth but poverty, not multitude but scarcity, not order but chaos — this is to lose what they desire and obtain what they avert.
text_normalized: But what they obtain is not wealth but poverty, not multitude but scarcity, not order but chaos — this is to lose what they desire and obtain what they avert.

text_original: Why is this?
text_normalized: Why is this?

text_original: Motse said: This is because the rulers have failed to exalt the virtuous and to employ the capable in their government.
text_normalized: Motse said: This is because the rulers have failed to exalt the virtuous and to employ the capable in their government.

text_original: When the virtuous are numerous in the state, order will be stable; when the virtuous are scarce, order will be unstable.
text_normalized: When the virtuous are numerous in the state, order will be stable; when the virtuous are scarce, order will be unstable.

text_original: Therefore the task of the lords lies nowhere but in multiplying the virtuous.
text_normalized: Therefore the task of the lords lies nowhere but in multiplying the virtuous.

text_original: But what is the way to multiply the virtuous?
text_normalized: But what is the way to multiply the virtuous?

text_original: Motse said: Supposing it is desired to multiply good archers and good drivers in the country, it will be only natural to enrich them, honotir them, respect them, and commend them; then good archers and good drivers can be expected to abound in the country.
text_normalized: Motse said: Supposing it is desired to multiply good archers and good drivers in the country, it will be only natural to enrich them, honotir them, respect them, and commend them; then good archers and good drivers can be expected to abound in the country.

text_original: How much more should this be done in the case of the virtuous and the excellent who are firm in morality, versed in rhetoric, and experienced in statecraft — since these are the treasures of the nation and props of the state?
text_normalized: How much more should this be done in the case of the virtuous and the excellent who are firm in morality, versed in rhetoric, and experienced in statecraft — since these are the treasures of the nation and props of the state?

text_original: They should also be enriched, honoured, respected, and commended in order that they may abound.
text_normalized: They should also be enriched, honoured, respected, and commended in order that they may abound.

text_original: When the ancient sage-kings administered the government they declared: " The unrighteous will not be enriched, the unrighteous will not be honoured, the unrighteous will not be favoured, the unrighteous will not be placed near."" Upon hearing this, the rich and honoured of the country all began to deliberate, saying: " What I have been depending on was wealth and honour.
text_normalized: When the ancient sage-kings administered the government they declared: " The unrighteous will not be enriched, the unrighteous will not be honoured, the unrighteous will not be favoured, the unrighteous will not be placed near."" Upon hearing this, the rich and honoured of the country all began to deliberate, saying: " What I have been depending on was wealth and honour.

text_original: Now the Lord promotes the righteous without discrimination against the poor and humble.
text_normalized: Now the Lord promotes the righteous without discrimination against the poor and humble.

text_original: Hence I may not do unrighteousness."" Upon hearing this, the favoured also began to deliberate, saying: " What I have been depending on was favour.
text_normalized: Hence I may not do unrighteousness."" Upon hearing this, the favoured also began to deliberate, saying: " What I have been depending on was favour.

text_original: Now the Lord promotes the righteous without discrimination against those thus far neglected.
text_normalized: Now the Lord promotes the righteous without discrimination against those thus far neglected.

text_normalized: Hence I may not do unrighteousness.

text_original: ' ’ Vassals of distant districts as well as youths in the palace, and multitudes within the state boundaries as well as the rustics living on the four borders, upon hearing this, all competed in doing righteousness.
text_normalized: ' ’ Vassals of distant districts as well as youths in the palace, and multitudes within the state boundaries as well as the rustics living on the four borders, upon hearing this, all competed in doing righteousness.

text_original: Now what is the reason for all this?
text_normalized: Now what is the reason for all this?

text_original: It is only with material goods that the superior can employ his subordinates, and it is only with statecraft that the sub-ordinates can serve their lord.
text_normalized: It is only with material goods that the superior can employ his subordinates, and it is only with statecraft that the sub-ordinates can serve their lord.

text_original: Take, for example, the rich man who built his walls high and left only one gate.
text_normalized: Take, for example, the rich man who built his walls high and left only one gate.

text_original: When the burglar had entered, the man closed the gate and searched for him, and the burglar had no more exit.
text_normalized: When the burglar had entered, the man closed the gate and searched for him, and the burglar had no more exit.

text_original: Because the man had the vantage-point.
text_normalized: Because the man had the vantage-point.

text_original: Therefore in administering the government, the ancient sage-kings ranked the morally excellent high and exalted the virtuous.
text_normalized: Therefore in administering the government, the ancient sage-kings ranked the morally excellent high and exalted the virtuous.

text_original: If capable, even a farmer or an artisan would be employed-commissioned with high rank, remunerated with liberal emoluments, trusted with important charges, and empowered to issue final orders.
text_normalized: If capable, even a farmer or an artisan would be employed-commissioned with high rank, remunerated with liberal emoluments, trusted with important charges, and empowered to issue final orders.

text_original: For, if his rank were not liigL, people would not respect him; if Ms emoluments were not liberal, people would not have confidence in him; if Ms orders were not final, people would not stand in awe before him.
text_normalized: For, if his rank were not liigL, people would not respect him; if Ms emoluments were not liberal, people would not have confidence in him; if Ms orders were not final, people would not stand in awe before him.

text_original: To place these three (honours) upon the virtuous is not so much to reward virtue, as to bring about the success of the enterprise (of government).
text_normalized: To place these three (honours) upon the virtuous is not so much to reward virtue, as to bring about the success of the enterprise (of government).

text_original: Therefore ranks should be standardized according to virtue, tasks assigned according to office, and rewards given according to labour spent.
text_normalized: Therefore ranks should be standardized according to virtue, tasks assigned according to office, and rewards given according to labour spent.

text_original: ’When emoluments are distributed in proportion to achievements, officials cannot be in constant honour, and people in eternal humility.
text_normalized: ’When emoluments are distributed in proportion to achievements, officials cannot be in constant honour, and people in eternal humility.

text_original: If a person is capable promote him, if incapable, lower his rank.
text_normalized: If a person is capable promote him, if incapable, lower his rank.

text_original: Give prominence to public approval and keep back private grudges (in the matter of selecting men).
text_normalized: Give prominence to public approval and keep back private grudges (in the matter of selecting men).

text_original: Here, then, is the principle.
text_normalized: Here, then, is the principle.
